note: One-time manual run — 12 leads (exception to standard 20-25 volume). All leads sourced from Midwest and regional publishers directories, Poets & Writers, and direct web verification.

leads.name: Finishing Line Press
leads.notes: Award-winning Georgetown, KY poetry press publishing since 1998 — annual New Women's Voices and New Men's Voices series create a predictable, recurring print cycle. Leah Maines is the named contact and decision-maker. Consistent short-run chapbook and collection production makes this a natural Cushing-Malloy account.

leads.name: Sarabande Books
leads.notes: Louisville, KY nonprofit literary press founded 1994 publishing poetry, short fiction, and essay — multiple award-winning titles annually with professional printing standards. Consistent catalog across 30+ years signals a stable, repeat-account printing opportunity. Verified contact via website.

leads.name: Dzanc Books
leads.notes: Ann Arbor-based nonprofit independent literary fiction publisher — literally Cushing-Malloy's neighbor. Active publishing schedule with professional production standards and a named contact (Michelle Dotter) who manages media and partnerships. Geographic alignment is the entire pitch.

leads.name: Mission Point Press
leads.notes: Traverse City, MI hybrid publisher specializing in Michigan regional stories, memoirs, and nonfiction — active publishing program with multiple titles annually and a growing client author base. Michigan-to-Michigan manufacturing partnership is a natural story. No phone number found via Apollo enrichment.

leads.name: Apex Book Company
leads.notes: Lexington, KY micro press founded 1997 specializing in science fiction, fantasy, and horror — owner Jason Sizemore is the sole decision-maker, simplifying the sales process. Genre readers demand premium hardcover and special edition quality, creating a clear upsell opportunity from POD to offset.

leads.name: Enchanted Lion Books
leads.notes: Brooklyn-based independent children's publisher producing 20-24 illustrated titles annually — high print volume, full-color production requirements, and a design-forward catalog that demands offset quality. Active and growing since 2003 with international author roster. No phone number found via Apollo enrichment.

leads.name: Haymarket Books
leads.notes: Chicago-based nonprofit publisher founded 2001, specializing in political and social justice nonfiction — growing catalog with consistent print demand and a loyal academic and activist readership. Regional proximity to Ann Arbor (3 hours) is a clear advantage. No phone number found via Apollo enrichment.

leads.name: Little Creek Press
leads.notes: Mineral Point, WI award-winning independent publisher founded 2011 — Kristin Mitchell has brought 200+ titles to life across children's books, memoirs, cookbooks, and poetry, building a loyal regional author community. Wisconsin location is within Cushing-Malloy's Midwest reach. No phone number found via Apollo enrichment.

leads.name: Beaver's Pond Press
leads.notes: Edina, MN hybrid publisher operating since 2000 — collaborative publishing model serves authors who want professional results without losing creative control. Multi-client model means consistent, recurring print orders across an active client base. Minnesota location is squarely in Cushing-Malloy's Midwest reach.

leads.name: Milkweed Editions
leads.notes: Minneapolis nonprofit literary publisher founded 1980 — 10-15 titles annually for adults and young adults, 40+ years of operations with national recognition. Morgan LaRocca (Publicity) is a verified direct contact. Midwest-to-Midwest manufacturing story is strong.

leads.name: Chicago Review Press
leads.notes: Founded 1973, Chicago-based independent publisher across 8 active imprints — consistently producing titles in nonfiction, history, and trade categories with a long-standing track record. Regional proximity (3 hours from Ann Arbor) and multi-imprint volume make them a compelling manufacturing conversation.

leads.name: BookComp, Inc.
leads.notes: Belmont, MI book composition and production services firm since 1989 — Jon Dertien (President) handles editorial, composition, and design for scholarly and trade titles. Natural referral partner: BookComp prepares the files, Cushing-Malloy manufactures the books. Direct Michigan-to-Michigan relationship.
